title: A square partitioned by a bright diagonal rule into two structural zones. Precept's contract carves the entire space of possible values into valid domains and structurally absent ones — there is no implicit path between them; only a declared transition can cross the boundary. The diagonal line is not a suggestion; it is the compiled rule.
conceptualBridge: The partition rule means Precept enforces state separation because the diagonal boundary is the compiled declaration that divides the valid domain from the structurally impossible one.
decision: Rejected: avg 3.81 below threshold — the diagonal partition reads as a generic 'split' or 'before/after' motif used by many products; monochrome test weak (two zones are similar tonal values); the concept is too abstract without strong specific anchoring to Precept.
